description: 1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

description: village in Cook County, Illinois United States of America
description_wiki: Brookfield (formerly Grossdale) is a village in Cook County, Illinois, United States, located 13 miles (21 km) west of downtown Chicago.

organization of U.S. war veterans
description_wiki: The American Legion, commonly known as the Legion, is an organization of U.S. veterans headquartered in Indianapolis, Indiana.

description: Roman Catholic religious order
description_wiki: The Servite Order, officially known as the Order of Servants of Mary (Latin: Ordo Servorum Beatae Mariae Virginis; abbreviated OSM), is one of the five original mendicant orders in the Catholic Church.

description: maritime branch of the United States military
description_wiki: The United States Navy (USN) is the maritime service branch of the United States Armed Forces and is designated as the navy of the United States in the Constitution.

description: theater of World War II fought in the Pacific and Asia
description_wiki: The Pacific War, sometimes called the Asia–Pacific War or the Pacific Theater, was the theater of World War II fought between Japan and the Allies in East and Southeast Asia, the Pacific and Indian Oceans, and Oceania.

description: city and county seat of Cook County, and largest city in State of Illinois, United States of America
description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.

description: private liberal arts college in Greencastle, Indiana, United States

description: city in Indiana, United States
description_wiki: Greencastle is a city in Greencastle Township, Putnam County, Indiana, United States, and the county seat of Putnam County.

description: US Navy program trained personnel in engineering, foreign languages, and medicine
description_wiki: The V-12 Navy College Training Program was designed to supplement the force of commissioned officers in the United States Navy during World War II. Between July 1, 1943, and June 30, 1946, more than 125,000 participants were enrolled in 131 colleges and universities in the United States.

description: public research university in West Lafayette, Indiana, United States
description_wiki: Purdue University is a public land-grant research university in West Lafayette, Indiana, United States, and the flagship campus of the Purdue University system, which also includes Purdue University in Indianapolis as an ongoing expansion of the West Lafayette campus.

description: private research university in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, United States
description_wiki: The University of Pennsylvania (Penn or UPenn) is a private Ivy League research university in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, United States.

description_wiki: The Naval Reserve Officer Training Corps (NROTC) program is a college-based, commissioned officer training program of the United States Navy and the United States Marine Corps.

aliases: Act to provide Federal Government aid for the readjustment in civilian life of returning World War II veterans
description: United States law that provided a range of benefits for returning World War II veterans
description_wiki: A G.I. Bill is a colloquial name given to several pieces of legislation that have provided a range of benefits for American military veterans, particularly education benefits.

description: American bank robber
description_wiki: John Herbert Dillinger (; June 22, 1903 – July 22, 1934) was an American gangster during the Great Depression.

description: president of the United States from 1861 to 1865 (1809-1865)
description_wiki: Abraham Lincoln (February 12, 1809 – April 15, 1865) was the 16th president of the United States, serving from 1861 until his assassination in 1865.
